factors.summary: Raw material and component costs are declining, benefiting gross profit.

evidence.text: Gross profit for the first quarter of fiscal 2016 increased $46.4, or 17.0%, to $319.4 compared with $273.0 in the prior-year period. Gross profit margin increased 120 basis points to 43.4% for the three months ended November 30, 2015 compared with 42.2% in the prior-year period. Gross profit was higher than the prior-year period due primarily to additional contribution on higher net sales as well as lower material and component costs.
evidence.subsection: First Quarter of Fiscal 2016 Compared with First Quarter of Fiscal 2015

factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable and increasing due to higher employee-related expenses.

evidence.text: The increase in SD&A expenses was due primarily to higher costs to support the greater sales volume, including freight and commissions, higher employee-related costs, including share-based incentive compensation, and increased amortization expense of acquired intangible assets.
evidence.subsection: First Quarter of Fiscal 2016 Compared with First Quarter of Fiscal 2015

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are expanding due to higher gross profit and cost management.

evidence.text: Gross profit margin increased 120 basis points to 43.4% for the three months ended November 30, 2015 compared with 42.2% in the prior-year period. Operating profit for the first quarter of fiscal 2016 was $112.4 compared with $86.7 for the prior-year period, an increase of $25.7, or 29.6%. Adjusted operating profit margin increased 110 basis points to 17.1% for the first quarter of fiscal 2016 compared with 16.0% for the year-ago period.
evidence.subsection: First Quarter of Fiscal 2016 Compared with First Quarter of Fiscal 2015

factors.summary: Cost reduction programs are delivering measurable savings, supporting margin expansion.

evidence.text: Adjusted operating profit increased by $22.2, or 21.4%, to $125.9 for the first quarter of fiscal 2016 compared with $103.7 for the first quarter of fiscal 2015. Adjusted operating profit margin increased 110 basis points to 17.1% for the first quarter of fiscal 2016 compared with 16.0% for the year-ago period.
evidence.subsection: First Quarter of Fiscal 2016 Compared with First Quarter of Fiscal 2015

factors.summary: Operational efficiency is improving, contributing to higher gross and operating margins.

evidence.subsection: First Quarter of Fiscal 2016 Compared with First Quarter of Fiscal 2015

factors.summary: Supply chain health is stable; no significant disruptions reported.

evidence.text: The Company’s cash position at November 30, 2015 was $560.2, a decrease of $196.6 from August 31, 2015. The decrease was due primarily to cash used to fund acquisitions of $239.2 and capital expenditures of $23.1 as well as pay dividends to stockholders of $5.7. These decreases were partially offset by cash flow generated from operations and stock issued under employee compensation plans.

As noted above, the Company expects to invest approximately two and a half percent of net sales primarily for equipment, tooling, facility enhancements, and new and enhanced information technology capabilities during fiscal 2016.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include unfavorable foreign currency rate changes and potential integration challenges from recent acquisitions.

evidence.text: Net sales were $736.6 for the three months ended November 30, 2015 compared with $647.4 reported for the three months ended November 30, 2014, an increase of $89.2, or 13.8%. The change in price/mix was due primarily to unfavorable pricing on LED luminaires, reflecting the decline in certain LED component costs, as well as a change in sales channel mix. Due to the changing dynamics of the Company's product portfolio, including the increase of integrated lighting solutions as well as the proliferation of new products due to the adoption of solid-state lighting, it is not possible to precisely quantify volume or differentiate the individual components of price/mix.
evidence.subsection: First Quarter of Fiscal 2016 Compared with First Quarter of Fiscal 2015

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Unfavorable foreign currency rates and integration challenges pose risks.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are expected to increase, signaling confidence in future growth.

evidence.text: The Company expects to invest approximately two and a half percent of net sales during fiscal 2016, of which $23.1 had been invested as of November 30, 2015, primarily for equipment, tooling, facility enhancements, as well as new and enhanced information technology capabilities.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Expected increase in capital expenditures indicates confidence in growth.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is stable, with total debt remaining unchanged and the debt to total capitalization ratio slightly decreasing.

evidence.text: As of November 30, 2015, total debt outstanding of $352.4 remained substantially unchanged from August 31, 2015 and consisted primarily of fixed-rate obligations. The debt to total capitalization ratio (calculated by dividing total debt by the sum of total debt and total stockholders’ equity) was 19.7% and 20.6% at November 30, 2015 and August 31, 2015, respectively.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Debt levels are stable with a slight decrease in the debt to capital ratio.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are maintained through dividend payments, with a moderate pace of dividend increases.

evidence.text: Acuity Brands paid dividends on its common stock of $5.7 and $5.6 ($0.13 per share) during the three months ended November 30, 2015 and 2014, respectively. All decisions regarding the declaration and payment of dividends by Acuity Brands are at the discretion of the Board and are evaluated regularly in light of the Company’s financial condition, earnings, growth prospects, funding requirements, applicable law, and any other factors the Board deems relevant.

sentiment.label: neutral

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating and expected to continue, driven by market growth, new product introductions, higher sales volumes, and expansion into underpenetrated geographies. This growth is supported by both recent performance and future strategic initiatives.

evidence.text: Management currently believes that the Company should benefit from recent acquisitions, further growth from the introduction of new lighting and building automation solutions, and expansion in underpenetrated geographies and channels.
Net sales were $736.6 for the three months ended November 30, 2015 compared with $647.4 reported for the three months ended November 30, 2014, an increase of $89.2, or 13.8%.
evidence.subsection: First Quarter of Fiscal 2016 Compared with First Quarter of Fiscal 2015

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong acceleration in organic growth driven by multiple factors.

factors.summary: The end markets for the company's lighting and energy management solutions are experiencing positive momentum, particularly in LED-based lighting and integrated systems. The North American lighting market is projected to grow at a mid-to-upper single-digit rate in fiscal 2016, with continued growth expected over the next several years.

evidence.text: The growth rate for the North American lighting market... is projected to be in the mid-to-upper single digit range for fiscal 2016 with continued growth over the next several years.

The Company is one of the world’s leading providers of lighting and energy management solutions for commercial, institutional, industrial, infrastructure, and residential applications... Additionally, the Company continues to expand its solutions portfolio for both indoor and outdoor applications in an effort to capitalize on the evolving and growing market for intelligent networked systems that collect and exchange data to increase efficiency as well as provide a host of other economic benefits resulting from data analytics to better enable smart buildings and smart cities.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Positive growth projections and market expansion opportunities.

factors.summary: Management is confident in the Company's strategy, acquisitions, and long-term growth prospects, as well as their ability to maintain strong cash flows and invest in future opportunities.

evidence.text: Management believes that the execution of the Company's strategy will provide opportunities for continued profitable growth... Management expects these acquisitions to be accretive to the Company's fiscal 2016 consolidated financial results.

The Company expects to invest approximately two and a half percent of net sales primarily for equipment, tooling, facility enhancements, and new and enhanced information technology capabilities during fiscal 2016. Additionally, management believes that the Company’s cash flows from operations and sources of funding, including, but not limited to, borrowing capacity, will sufficiently support the long-term liquidity needs of the Company.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Confidence in strategy, acquisitions, and future growth.

factors.summary: The company's near-term results are influenced by energy and environmental concerns, market demand, and the integration of acquisitions. Additionally, foreign currency rate changes and the success of integration efforts are key sensitivities impacting performance.

evidence.text: The Company’s addressable markets will experience solid growth... particularly as energy and environmental concerns come to the forefront... The Company's ability to realize benefits from initiatives... and timing of benefits.

The change in price/mix was due primarily to unfavorable pricing on LED luminaires, reflecting the decline in certain LED component costs, as well as a change in sales channel mix. Due to the changing dynamics of the Company's product portfolio, including the increase of integrated lighting solutions as well as the proliferation of new products due to the adoption of solid-state lighting, it is not possible to precisely quantify volume or differentiate the individual components of price/mix.
evidence.subsection: First Quarter of Fiscal 2016 Compared with First Quarter of Fiscal 2015

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Identified sensitivities but no clear negative impact.

factors.summary: The company has been actively engaging in acquisition activity, which has expanded its product portfolio and capabilities, particularly in lighting solutions and building automation. These acquisitions are expected to be accretive to fiscal 2016 results, indicating a positive direction for the company's growth strategy.

evidence.text: In September 2015, the Company completed the acquisition of Distech Controls... Management expects these acquisitions to be accretive to the Company's fiscal 2016 consolidated financial results.

The Company continues to expand and enhance its portfolio of lighting solutions, including the following transactions: On December 10, 2015, using cash on hand, the Company acquired all of the equity interests of Juno Lighting LLC (''Juno Lighting''), a leading provider of downlighting and track lighting fixtures for both residential and commercial applications. Juno Lighting is headquartered in Des Plaines, Illinois. On December 9, 2015, using cash on hand, the Company acquired all of the equity interests of Geometri, LLC (''Geometri''), a provider of a software and services platform for mapping, navigation, and analytics. On September 1, 2015, using cash on hand, the Company acquired all of the outstanding capital stock of Distech Controls Inc. (''Distech Controls''), a provider of building automation solutions that allow for the integration of lighting, HVAC, access control, closed circuit television, and related systems. Distech Controls is headquartered in Quebec, Canada. On April 15, 2015, the Company acquired for cash substantially all of the assets and assumed certain liabilities of ByteLight, Inc. (''ByteLight''), a provider of indoor location software for LED lighting. ByteLight is headquartered in Boston, Massachusetts. In addition, during fiscal 2015, the Company made a strategic, non-controlling investment in a company specializing in light sensory networks.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Active and successful acquisition activity supporting growth.
